page: Microsoft Azure E32d_v5 is an x86_64 architecture memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The processor is equipped with 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It also includes 1229 GB of local NVMe storage, offering a bundled storage solution for workloads requiring fast temporary disk access. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

Performance benchmarks place the E32d_v5 in the average tier for most compute tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 31085.46 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13826.0. However, memory performance is a notable strength, with the PassMark memory score rated as strong (top 25%) and a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1679.85 GB/sec. For language model tasks, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 280.92 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, though it drops to a weak 6.65 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model, highlighting its limitations for heavy LLM workloads without GPU acceleration.

Given its high memory density and bundled local storage, the E32d_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that do not require dedicated GPU hardware. It is well-suited for relational database servers, in-memory caching systems like Redis (which benchmarked at 31.39 million operations per second), data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E32d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv5 family, powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 32 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it highly effective for memory-bound workloads. Benchmark results show strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and small-block read bandwidth reaching 1679.85 GB/sec. While general compute and database operations perform in the average tier, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds for smaller language models. Lacking a GPU, it is not suited for large-scale AI model execution. The E32d_v5 is best suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM and local temporary storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage. It delivers strong memory performance for databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-density RAM and bundled local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 vCPUs and 2.8 GHz clock speed
High-capacity 256.0 GB RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Bundled 1229 GB of local storage for fast temporary data access
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of PassMark benchmarks
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
